category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems

surface.name: Unity Manual: Create your first project, using the Get Started with Unity learning template
surface.selection_basis: The current Unity Manual has a page explicitly titled Create your first project for first-time users. It requires a Unity ID, eligible plan and active license, Hub, Editor 6.1 or later, and a template-based project. The page names Get Started with Unity as the template that introduces core concepts, so this record instantiates the otherwise unspecified template choice with that first-time-user candidate.

alternatives_considered.name: Any Core, Sample, or Learning project template
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The generic Hub procedure requires a template but does not select one; the first-time-user manual specifically calls out Get Started with Unity.

alternatives_considered.name: Get Started with Unity: In-Editor Tutorial
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This 45-minute learning exercise continues after project creation through five in-Editor tutorial sections to a playable game and optional sharing, while the manual's first named milestone is project creation.

alternatives_considered.name: Unity Gaming Services quickstarts
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: These are service-specific paths for Analytics, Authentication, Cloud Code, Multiplayer, Economy, and other cloud products rather than the general Unity Engine first-project route.

entry_point.developer_intent: Install the general Unity creation environment and create the first local Unity project from the first-time-user learning template.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has no Unity account, Unity Hub, active Unity license, Unity Editor, or project.
The developer is using a supported Windows, macOS, or Linux workstation or laptop and has permission to install software and write a local project.
No paid plan, cloud service, source-control provider, build-target module, or publishing account is assumed.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Create a Unity ID with a default organization and eligible subscription, install and sign in to Unity Hub, activate a license, install Unity Editor 6.1 or later, select the Get Started with Unity learning template, name and locate a project, and create it so it appears in the Projects list and opens in the Unity Editor.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The Hub adds the named project to the Projects list and opens it in the Unity Editor; with the selected learning template, the Editor displays the tutorial welcome screen with a robot greeting the developer.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The current first-time-user manual is titled Create your first project and its ordered creation path ends at Create project. The detailed Hub page says this creates the project, adds it to the Projects list, and opens it in the Editor.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: A local project opened in the Editor is the terminal state of Unity's named first-project procedure. Completing the five guided learning sections creates a playable game, but the manual places learning after project creation under Continue learning.

prerequisites.requirement: A workstation or laptop that meets the selected Unity Editor version's operating-system, CPU, graphics, driver, and memory requirements; the Unity 6 requirements recommend at least 8 GB RAM.
Permission to download and install Unity Hub and Editor, grant filesystem and firewall access when requested, and write to the chosen local project location.

A Unity ID for Hub sign-in. The direct account route requires an email, password, Unity Cloud username, full name, terms and privacy choices, and selection of Create Unity ID; Unity then associates a default organization and free Unity Personal subscription. Third-party and company-SSO identity branches are also documented.

An eligible Unity plan. Unity Personal is free and requires no credit card for eligible hobbyists, individuals, and small organizations below the published $200,000 revenue or funding threshold; Pro, Enterprise, Industry, and other plan rules apply when the developer or organization is outside Personal eligibility.

An active Unity license associated with the signed-in account; Unity Personal can be automatically activated or explicitly acquired for free, while organizational licenses require a seat or serial route.
Unity Hub and Unity Editor version 6.1 or later; the selected current learning template exercise calls for Unity 6.3 or later.
Network access for account sign-in, license activation, Hub and Editor downloads, and template download.

primary_path.action: Open the current Unity Manual page Create your first project.
details: Treat its Install the Unity Editor and Create a project sections as the ordered general first-time-user path.
primary_path.input: Intent to begin with Unity Engine.
primary_path.success_signal: The two required sections and Continue learning boundary are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: None documented.

primary_path.action: Check that the developer machine meets the Unity system requirements.
details: Select the requirements for the intended Editor version and operating system.
Unity documents CPU, graphics API and driver, operating-system, form-factor, and memory constraints.
primary_path.input: Machine operating system and hardware specifications.
primary_path.success_signal: The machine satisfies the selected Editor version's requirements.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Unsupported operating system, emulation, CPU, graphics, driver, or insufficient resources blocks or degrades the Editor.

primary_path.action: Open Unity Dashboard to begin account creation.
details: Unity's account guide names Unity Dashboard as the account-creation entry point.
primary_path.success_signal: Create account is visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Dashboard, network, or browser-policy failures block the account route.

primary_path.action: Select Create account.
details: Existing Unity ID holders can instead sign in and continue to the plan and Hub steps.
primary_path.success_signal: The identity and policy fields are displayed.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A developer who cannot access the form must use an available sign-in or recovery route.

primary_path.action: Enter the account email, password, Unity Cloud username, and full name.
details: The password is omitted on the company-SSO branch because Unity does not set one during SSO account creation.
primary_path.input: Email, password, desired Unity Cloud username, and full name.
primary_path.success_signal: The form accepts all four required values.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid, unavailable, or noncompliant values prevent account submission.

primary_path.action: Make the documented terms, privacy, and promotional-update choices.
details: Choose whether to accept the terms of service, acknowledge the privacy policies, and opt in to promotional updates.
primary_path.output: Recorded terms, privacy, and promotion choices.
primary_path.success_signal: Required policy choices permit submission.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Declining a required policy prevents account creation; promotional updates are an opt-in choice.

primary_path.action: Select Create Unity ID.
details: This submits the completed direct-account form.
primary_path.success_signal: Unity accepts the request and creates the account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Form rejection or any live post-submit verification delays account access.

primary_path.action: Create the Unity ID, associate its default organization, and add a free Unity Personal subscription to that organization.
details: Unity documents these organization and subscription results as automatic consequences of account creation.
primary_path.output: Unity ID, default organization, and Unity Personal subscription.
primary_path.success_signal: The account can access Unity products and its default organization has a Personal subscription.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Provisioning failure or an inaccessible organization prevents later license activation.

primary_path.action: Confirm that Unity Personal is the eligible plan for the developer or organization.
details: Unity Personal is free and the current plans page says no credit card is required.

For gaming and entertainment use, the published Personal eligibility generally requires less than $200,000 USD in revenue or funds raised in the prior 12 months, with additional client-aggregate rules for service providers.
Businesses above $200,000 can require Pro, those above $25 million require Enterprise, and non-game or non-entertainment businesses above $1 million in total finances require Industry.
primary_path.input: Use case, business finances, client finances when providing services, and desired plan features.
primary_path.output: Eligible Personal route or selected paid-plan branch.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer has a plan that satisfies Unity's published eligibility rules.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Using Personal outside its eligibility rules requires moving to the applicable Pro, Enterprise, or Industry plan before relying on the license.

primary_path.action: Go to Unity's Download Unity page.
details: Use the platform-specific Hub installer path documented for Windows or macOS; use the repository instructions for Linux.
primary_path.input: Supported operating system, eligible plan, Unity ID, and network access.
primary_path.output: Hub download or Linux repository instructions.
primary_path.success_signal: The appropriate stable Hub installation option is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Unsupported Linux distributions are not guaranteed to work.

primary_path.action: Download the Unity Hub installer for Windows or macOS.
details: Select the platform download from the Unity download page.
primary_path.input: Unity download page and authenticated account when requested.
primary_path.success_signal: The installer is present on the machine.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Download, proxy, storage, or browser policy can prevent retrieval.

primary_path.action: Open the Hub installer and follow the setup instructions.
details: Use the .exe on Windows or .dmg on macOS.
primary_path.success_signal: Unity Hub launches.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Windows Mandatory ASLR, registry state, install path, network-drive paths, administrator policy, or macOS security controls can block installation; the Hub page documents troubleshooting for several of these cases.

primary_path.action: Sign in to Unity Hub with the Unity ID.
details: Grant requested filesystem or firewall access needed for local projects, Editor installations, and cloud resources.
primary_path.success_signal: The signed-in Hub navigation is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Authentication, firewall, proxy, or permission denial prevents Hub resources from loading.

primary_path.action: Automatically activate the signed-in developer's Unity Personal license when eligible.
details: Unity says no further action is required when Personal activation succeeds at sign-in.
primary_path.output: Active Personal license associated with the machine.
primary_path.success_signal: Licenses shows an active Unity Personal license.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If no license appears, use Licenses > Add license > Get a free personal license, read the terms, and agree; paid organizational accounts follow different branches.

primary_path.action: Open Installs and select Install Editor.
details: The Hub shows Official releases, Pre-releases, and Archive routes.
primary_path.success_signal: Installable official Editor versions appear.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Network or account state can prevent the release catalog from loading.

primary_path.action: Choose an official Unity Editor version 6.3 or later and select Install.
details: The general first-project manual requires 6.1 or later; the current Get Started with Unity learning exercise requires 6.3 or later, so 6.3 or later satisfies both.
primary_path.success_signal: Module-selection window opens.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The selected version controls which templates are available and must be compatible with the learning template.

primary_path.action: Review and select any Editor modules, then select Install.
details: Modules add build support, language packs, and developer tools and can also be added later; no extra module is named for simply opening the selected learning project.
primary_path.success_signal: Download appears in the Hub Downloads panel.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Module choice can add storage, download, SDK, and platform requirements.

primary_path.action: Download and install the Unity Editor and selected modules.
details: Monitor download and installation progress from the Hub toolbar.
primary_path.input: Editor installation job, network, storage, and install permissions.
primary_path.success_signal: The installation appears in the Installs panel and the download group reports completion.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The Hub exposes progress details and a cancellation control; no installation duration is documented.

primary_path.action: Open the Hub, select Projects, and select New project.
details: The New project window is the manual's creation surface.
primary_path.success_signal: Editor version, template, name, location, and source-control controls appear.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Project creation requires an active license associated with the account.

primary_path.action: Select the installed Unity Editor version 6.3 or later.
details: Changing Editor version updates the compatible template list.
primary_path.input: New project window and installed Editors.
primary_path.success_signal: The selected version appears at the top of the window.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The desired template may not be available for an incompatible Editor version.

primary_path.action: Open the Learning template category and select Get Started with Unity.
details: Learning templates include guided tutorials, sample scenes, and assets; the selected template is Unity's first-time-user learning candidate.
primary_path.input: Compatible Editor version and template catalog.
primary_path.output: Get Started with Unity project template selected.
primary_path.success_signal: The template detail is selected in New project.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The generic first-project manual does not require this exact template, and template availability depends on Editor version.

primary_path.action: If the template is not present locally, select Download template and wait for completion.
details: The create control should be used only after the template download completes.
primary_path.input: Selected undownloaded template and network access.
primary_path.success_signal: Template download is complete and project creation is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Network, disk, account, or version errors can block the template download; no duration is documented.

primary_path.action: Enter a project name.
details: The Hub uses the name for the folder that stores assets, scenes, and other project files.
primary_path.success_signal: A valid project name is present.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid or conflicting names can prevent creation.

primary_path.action: Choose a local project location.
details: The default is the user's home directory; choose another local folder if needed.
The Unity Manual warns that cloud-based storage is unsupported and can cause synchronization issues that corrupt projects.
primary_path.success_signal: A writable non-cloud-synchronized location is selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Filesystem permissions, insufficient disk, or unsupported cloud synchronization can prevent or corrupt project creation.

primary_path.action: Leave Select source control provider unset for the baseline local project.
details: Creating a Unity Version Control, GitHub, or GitLab repository is optional and adds external credentials and repository configuration.
primary_path.success_signal: No remote provider is selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Choosing a provider invokes separate token, permission, namespace, and remote-repository steps.

primary_path.action: Select Create project.
details: The Hub generates the project from the selected template.
primary_path.input: Valid Editor, template, name, location, active license, and local permissions.
primary_path.success_signal: The Hub begins creating the project.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: On Windows, the current Hub documentation says to close the Hub and run it as administrator if project creation errors occur.

primary_path.action: Create the project, add it to the Hub Projects list, and open it in Unity Editor.
details: For the selected learning template, Unity Learn says creation takes a few minutes and the Editor then shows a robot welcome screen.
primary_path.success_signal: The named project appears in the Projects list and the Editor opens the project; the robot welcome screen confirms the tutorial template is ready.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wait for generation and import; Unity Learn states a few minutes for this template. Creation or Editor-launch errors require environment, permission, license, storage, template, or Windows administrator diagnosis.

branches.condition: The developer signs in to the Unity Editor or Unity website with a third-party account such as Google or Facebook.
branches.path: Complete the third-party authentication handoff. Unity creates a Unity ID for that identity; confirm the resulting organization and eligible subscription, then continue to Hub installation and sign-in.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces direct email, password, username, full-name, and Create Unity ID form submission with an external identity-provider flow.

branches.condition: The developer creates the account through company SSO.
branches.path: Complete the company's SSO account-creation flow. Unity does not set a password during this route; for a later password-based sign-in, select Forgot your password? and create one.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces the direct password field with an enterprise identity handoff and can add company identity-policy or administrator gates.

branches.condition: Revenue or funding exceeds the Unity Personal threshold but does not require Enterprise or Industry.
branches.path: Choose Unity Pro, complete its paid subscription or organizational membership flow, obtain a named-user seat or other applicable license entitlement, and activate it through Hub sign-in or the documented license route.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces the no-credit-card Personal route with purchase, organization, seat, and license-assignment work.

branches.condition: The business has more than $25 million USD in annual revenue or funding.
branches.path: Use Unity Enterprise, complete the sales and custom-pricing path, join the subscribing organization, receive an assigned seat or approved floating-license route, then activate the applicable license.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds sales, contract, organization, administrator, seat or licensing-server, and wait gates before the project journey.

branches.condition: The organization creates applications outside games or entertainment and its total finances exceed $1 million USD.
branches.path: Use Unity Industry, contact Sales for custom pricing and subscription setup, join the entitled organization, receive an assigned license, and activate it in the Hub before creating the project.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces Personal with an Industry sales, contract, organization, and license-assignment path.

branches.path: Install curl if absent, add Unity's public signing key, add the stable amd64 Hub repository, run apt update, and install unityhub; RHEL, CentOS, and Fedora have a separate unsupported RPM route.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces the Windows or macOS graphical installer with package-manager, signing-key, repository, sudo, architecture, and filesystem-permission steps.

branches.condition: Unity Personal does not automatically activate at sign-in.
branches.path: Select Licenses > Add license > Get a free personal license, read the terms, and select Agree and get personal edition license.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds explicit plan choice and terms acceptance before project creation.

branches.condition: The developer uses a named-user organizational plan.
branches.path: An organization administrator must add the developer to the subscribing organization and assign a seat, after which Hub sign-in automatically activates the license.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds external administrator, membership, entitlement, and possible wait gates.

branches.condition: The developer uses a serial-based plan.
branches.path: Select Licenses > Add license > Activate with a serial number, enter the serial key, and select Activate; offline serial activation uses the documented 14-action two-computer request-file journey.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds credential or offline file-transfer and portal work before the Editor can create a project.

branches.condition: The developer installs an archived or manually downloaded Editor.
branches.path: Use the Archive tab and download-archive Hub link, or select Locate and identify the existing Editor executable.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes version retrieval and may introduce unsupported-version or compatibility risk.

branches.condition: The developer chooses a different Core, Sample, or Learning template.
branches.path: Select the desired compatible template, review its supported platforms and packages, download it if needed, and continue with the same name, location, and Create project flow.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The first project still opens, but its content, packages, download size, platform assumptions, and completion signal differ.

branches.condition: The developer connects source control during creation.
branches.path: Choose Unity Version Control, GitHub, or GitLab and complete the provider-specific repository, token, scope, authentication, ownership, visibility, description, and branch configuration documented on the project-creation page.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an external account, credential, permission, and remote-repository journey before Create project.

friction_gates.description: The machine must satisfy Unity Editor operating-system, CPU, GPU, driver, memory, and non-emulated workstation or laptop requirements.
Unity ID creation or an existing direct, third-party, or company-SSO identity is required for the Hub route.
The direct account route requires email, password, Unity Cloud username, and full name; company SSO and third-party sign-in alter these fields.
Account creation requires terms-of-service and privacy-policy choices and exposes an optional promotional-updates choice.
Unity plan eligibility depends on use case, revenue, funding, client finances for service providers, and the published Personal, Pro, Enterprise, and Industry thresholds.
Eligible Unity Personal use is free and requires no credit card; Pro, Enterprise, and Industry routes add paid subscription or sales steps.
Software-install, filesystem, firewall, and sometimes administrator permissions are required.
An active Personal, named-user, serial, education, or floating Unity license must be associated with the account and machine.
Named-user plans require an organization administrator to assign a seat before Hub sign-in can activate the license.
The Editor and any selected modules require a potentially large download, local storage, and install completion before a project can be created.
The generic first-project manual requires a template but does not mandate one; template availability and content depend on the selected Editor version.
An undownloaded project template must finish downloading before project creation.
A writable local location is required; Unity warns that cloud-synchronized project storage is unsupported and may corrupt a project.
Unity creates, imports, and opens the project; Unity Learn states that the selected learning-template creation takes a few minutes.

excluded_after_success: Completing all five sections of the in-Editor tutorial to create a simple playable game; this is the next learning milestone after the first project opens.
The optional 45-minute Unity Learn exercise completion and optional Unity Play or Learn-platform sharing, submission metadata, visibility, preview, CAPTCHA, and publishing.
Unity Essentials topics covering 2D and 3D fundamentals, programming, audio, cameras, visual effects, animation, lighting, UI, iteration, certification, and publishing.
Build-target modules, player builds, code signing, app stores, Unity Gaming Services, Cloud, Analytics, Ads, Multiplayer, source control, CI, deployment, and production operations.

uncertainties.question: Which template defines the general Unity first mile?
uncertainties.impact: Template selection changes downloads, packages, default scenes, target platforms, and the first observable Editor content.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The current Create your first project manual requires selecting a template and calls out Get Started with Unity as an educational option, but it does not explicitly require that template or a Core alternative.

uncertainties.question: Should Unity's first success be an opened project or the playable game after all five in-Editor tutorial sections?
uncertainties.impact: The latter adds the entire 45-minute guided Editor experience and many unexposed in-product actions.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The Manual explicitly names Create your first project and separates Continue learning, while Unity Learn describes its longer exercise as completing the first Unity project and making a simple playable game.

uncertainties.question: Does the live direct-account flow add email confirmation, anti-abuse verification, or another post-submit screen before the Unity ID is usable?
uncertainties.impact: Any additional live verification would add an access or wait step before Hub sign-in.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The current official account guide documents the complete form, policy choices, Create Unity ID action, default organization, and Personal subscription, but does not describe a post-submit verification screen.

uncertainties.question: Which operating system, eligible organizational license route, Editor release, and modules apply to the developer?
uncertainties.impact: These choices produce different installer actions, administrator involvement, downloads, permissions, SDKs, and waits.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official general path branches across Windows, macOS, Linux, Personal, named-user, serial, education, floating, current, archived, and module-specific routes; the roster supplies no developer or organization state.

sources.title: Create your first project
evidence_supported: Named first-project boundary, system check, Hub, account, Editor 6.1+, project actions, template callout, local-storage warning, and post-success learning

sources.title: System requirements for Unity 6.0
evidence_supported: Representative Unity 6 machine, operating system, graphics, driver, memory, form-factor, and platform constraints

sources.title: Install the Hub on Windows or macOS
sections_used: Install the Hub on Windows or macOS
evidence_supported: Account, installer download and execution, Hub sign-in, permissions, supported systems, and troubleshooting

sources.title: Install the Unity Hub on Linux
sections_used: Install the Hub on an Ubuntu Linux distribution
Install the Hub on Red Hat Enterprise Linux, CentOS, or Fedora
evidence_supported: Linux distribution, curl, signing key, repository, package, sudo, support-status, and troubleshooting branch

sources.title: Download and install the Unity Editor
sections_used: Install an archived Unity Editor version
Add a manually downloaded Editor to the Hub
evidence_supported: Editor release, module, install, progress, archived, and local-existing routes

sources.title: Managing licenses
sections_used: Activate a license for a Unity Personal plan
Get a free license for Unity Personal
evidence_supported: Active-license requirement and Personal, administrator seat, serial, and offline activation branches

sources.title: Create a new project
sections_used: Create a project from a template
Create a source control repository for a project
evidence_supported: License gate, complete project form, template download wait, source-control branch, creation failure, and terminal Projects-list and Editor-open state

sources.title: Get Started with Unity: In-Editor Tutorial
sections_used: Open Unity Hub and launch the project
evidence_supported: Version 6.3+, learning template selection, project-open few-minute wait, robot welcome signal, five-section later journey, 45-minute exercise label, and playable-game alternative boundary

sources.title: Create an account
evidence_supported: Unity Dashboard entry, Create account, email, password, Unity Cloud username, full name, terms, privacy, promotional choice, Create Unity ID, automatic default organization and Personal subscription, third-party sign-in, and company SSO password behavior

sources.title: Unity Personal
sections_used: Can I use Unity Personal for commercial development?
evidence_supported: Unity Personal is free, its under-$200,000 revenue and funding eligibility, service-provider client threshold, business-plan handoff, and Pro requirement for closed-platform deployment

sources.title: Unity Plans and Pricing
sections_used: What Unity plan am I eligible to use?
evidence_supported: Personal is free with no credit card, Pro is required above $200,000, Enterprise above $25 million, Industry for non-game or non-entertainment work above $1 million in total finances, and paid-plan sales and licensing branches
